action.hypothesis: After three non-improving rounds, the highest-value exploitation move is to keep the current best family fixed on the more valuable high-quality smaller-K anchor `K=96` and sweep learning rate. The specific hypothesis is that lowering `LR` from `3.2e-3` to `1.6e-3` for `routed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` may improve optimization stability enough to beat the current `K=96` frontier point without giving up the stronger Pareto tradeoff relative to `K=128`.
action.summary: Run one parameter-only exploitation experiment at `K=96` with `routed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual`, keeping `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, and `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` fixed while changing only `LR` from `3.2e-3` to `1.6e-3`. Success criterion: beat the current `K=96` frontier anchor FVU (`0.06254687905311584`) and determine whether the routed family benefits from a lower LR at the most valuable high-quality smaller-K slot.
action.self_review: This follows the explicit stagnation guidance to switch into exploitation on the current best family and changes exactly one parameter. I chose `K=96` instead of `K=128` because the smaller-K high-quality point is the stronger Pareto target if quality can be maintained or improved. The main risk is that `3.2e-3` was already near-optimal for this family and lowering LR will slow progress or underfit.
notes_to_memory: Stagnation policy triggered after three non-improving rounds, so round 102 should switch to exploitation on the current best routed family rather than continue plain-family cleanup.
Choose `K=96` as the exploitation anchor because it is a more valuable Pareto point than `K=128` if FVU improves under the same family.
Keep all recipe fields fixed except `LR`; this is the first routed-family LR sweep point and tests whether lower LR improves the high-quality smaller-K lane.
next_hypotheses: If `LR=1.6e-3` wins at `K=96`, propagate the same LR to `K=128` under the fixed routed recipe to see whether the improvement generalizes to the top-quality anchor.
If it is close but still behind at `K=96`, test the next lower routed-family LR (`4e-4`) before switching dimensions.
If it clearly loses at `K=96`, restore `LR=3.2e-3` as the routed baseline and move the next exploitation round to `AUXK_ALPHA=0.01` at the same `K=96` anchor.

result.description: Run one parameter-only exploitation experiment at `K=96` with `routed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual`, keeping `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, and `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` fixed while changing only `LR` from `3.2e-3` to `1.6e-3`. Success criterion: beat the current `K=96` frontier anchor FVU (`0.06254687905311584`) and determine whether the routed family benefits from a lower LR at the most valuable high-quality smaller-K slot.

I chose `K=96` instead of `K=128` because the smaller-K high-quality point is the stronger Pareto target if quality can be maintained or improved. The main risk is that `3.2e-3` was already near-optimal for this family and lowering LR will slow progress or underfit.
